pipeline_components.architect: EdogQaLlmClient.ArchitectOnceAsync (gpt-5.4, high reasoning, strict json_schema, T2 prompt: GRANULARITY RULE + ANTI-QUOTA GUARD + PR-TYPE CATEGORY HEURISTIC + CATEGORY PRECEDENCE + CONTRACT-BEARING COMMENTS; ArchitectMaxOutputTokens=96000) — UNCHANGED from T2
pipeline_components.editor: EdogQaLlmClient.EditorOnceAsync (gpt-5.4-mini, low reasoning, strict json_schema, T2 prompt: STRICT 1:1 SKETCH-TO-SCENARIO MAPPING + ARCHITECT-LABEL PRESERVATION + CATEGORY SELECTION GUIDE; EditorMaxOutputTokens=32000) — UNCHANGED from T2
pipeline_components.scorer: tests/qa-eval/score_eval.py --span-expansion 15 --matcher bipartite (T4-A: category-cluster matcher via tests/qa-eval/category_aliases.json — {HappyPath, EdgeCase, ErrorPath, Regression} cluster into 'behavioral' for cardinality; Performance stays separate; category_label_accuracy reported as a separate diagnostic of matched-pair raw-label agreement; span_expansion N=15 retained from T1j)

notes: T4-A (matcher-side category-cluster calibration) — OFFLINE win, no LLM cost. The T4-A matcher sensitivity audit (tests/qa-eval/matcher_audit.py + matcher_audit_drift.py + matcher_audit_validate.py) measured a +19 pp gap between strict raw-label category matching (0.577) and category-cluster matching (0.767) on the IDENTICAL T2 captures, then hand-validated 11/11 newly-recovered pairs as TRUE semantic equivalents — pairs where the LLM grounds at the same file/line as the curator but uses a different category lens (curator describes WHAT BEHAVIOUR is being tested; Architect describes WHAT KIND OF CODE PATH the diff introduces). The matcher's job is scenario-level equivalence, so {HappyPath, EdgeCase, ErrorPath, Regression} cluster into one 'behavioral' bucket; Performance stays in its own cluster. category_label_accuracy (NEW) reports the fraction of matched pairs that agree on the raw label = 0.622 on n=6 (Architect picks the curator's exact label 62%% of the time after a successful match). Lift over T2: macro_recall 0.577 -> 0.767 (+33%% relative, +0.190 absolute), macro_precision_highest 0.502 -> 0.666 (+33%% relative, +0.164 absolute), macro_F1_highest 0.527 -> 0.701 (+33%% relative). Per-PR ratchets: PR-955910 0.333 -> 0.583 (+25 pp), PR-960543 0.364 -> 0.818 (+45 pp), PR-966141 0.750 (flat — already at the calibration ceiling), PR-975848 0.750 (flat), PR-976609 0.667 -> 1.000 (+33 pp, FIRST PR AT PERFECT RECALL), PR-977882 0.600 -> 0.700 (+10 pp). Spec target 0.80 macro_recall: 0.033 absolute away. Remaining headroom: drop-both ceiling is 0.85, so verb-cluster aliasing (T4-B, also offline) carries ~0.08 more recall; corpus expansion to n>=15 (T4-C) is the final lever. T3 (FLT-priors prefix block) was REVERTED in-session — unconditional injection regressed macro_recall to 0.493 (-14.6%%) by hurting surgical small PRs more than it lifted dense ones; conditional gate (>=5 files) added complexity that didn't survive paid-capture variance. Lesson: matcher calibration > prompt tuning when the corpus is small (n=6) and the matcher has untested constraints. The T4-A audit script (tests/qa-eval/matcher_audit.py) is a reusable diagnostic and should be re-run after every matcher change. See score_report.json (schema 1.4, T4-A) for the verdict block.

errors: Architect chronically exhausted 65K budget at T2 prompt density — required ArchitectMaxOutputTokens=96000 bump to capture (T2 baseline note retained).
Architect capture variance-prone at 96K (T2 baseline note retained). Editor required EditorMaxOutputTokens=32000 bump to fit 10-sketch dense scenario set.
